steps.title: Write out the addition
steps.content: The sum is $(4x^4 + 3x^3) + (5x^4 + 6x^3)$. Group terms by their exponent.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $x^{14}$ — Exponents are added as if multiplying, not adding polynomials. Eliminated.
  • B: $x^8 + 9x^6$ — Exponents are doubled, not like terms combined. Eliminated.
  • C: $x^4 + 18x^3$ — The $x^3$ coefficient 18 comes from $3\cdot6$ rather than $3+6$. Eliminated.
  • D: $9x^4 + 9x^3$ — The coefficients are correct ($4+5=9$ and $3+6=9$). That is the correct answer.
